summary: Plan-only PR inventory classification. No shared canonical was selected. Security-sensitive candidates are routed; non-security candidates are kept open unless a specific maintainer/security/review blocker requires human judgment.

actions.reason: Open candidate with failed proof and no duplicate/supersession evidence; keep as an independent PR for normal review.
This is a maintainer/product correctness decision, not a dedupe or low-signal closeout.
Useful maintained PR with passing proof but dirty merge state; no close action is justified in plan mode.
Focused candidate with proof but unresolved review/change state; keep open independently.
The open question is whether to accept a proof override; that is maintainer judgment.
No duplicate or close path is established; unresolved review bot feedback blocks stronger action.
Security-sensitive candidate must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling and not triaged through backlog cleanup.
SSRF/private network restriction changes are security-sensitive and out of ProjectClownfish cleanup scope.
Tool/system-prompt surface with security-boundary labeling is routed to central handling.
Keep the focused non-security PR open for normal proof/maintainer review; no allowed close or merge action is supported.

needs_human: #85229 requires maintainer/product judgment on whether the host mutation denial approach is technically useful given review concerns about exec/Codex bypass paths.
#85241 requires maintainer judgment on the author's requested proof override after failed real behavior proof.
